_comment: Run: python execution/download_corpus.py --persona alexander-hamilton. All sources are public domain — Hamilton died in 1804. The Lodge 12-volume set is the comprehensive source; individual reports are also targeted separately as they may appear as standalone scans.

priority_1.title: The Federalist Papers
priority_1.author: Alexander Hamilton, James Madison, John Jay
priority_1._note: Complete collection of all 85 papers. Hamilton authored 51 (Nos. 1, 6-9, 11-13, 15-17, 21-36, 59-61, 65-85). The definitive statement of his constitutional and political theory.

priority_1.title: Works of Alexander Hamilton Vol 1 (Lodge Edition)
priority_1.author: Alexander Hamilton

priority_1.title: Works of Alexander Hamilton Vol 2 (Lodge Edition)
priority_1.author: Alexander Hamilton

priority_1.title: Works of Alexander Hamilton Vol 3 (Lodge Edition)
priority_1.author: Alexander Hamilton

priority_1.title: Works of Alexander Hamilton Vol 4 (Lodge Edition)
priority_1.author: Alexander Hamilton

priority_1.title: Works of Alexander Hamilton Vol 5 (Lodge Edition)
priority_1.author: Alexander Hamilton

priority_1.title: Works of Alexander Hamilton Vol 6 (Lodge Edition)
priority_1.author: Alexander Hamilton

priority_1.title: Report on Public Credit
priority_1.author: Alexander Hamilton
priority_1.search_terms: hamilton report on public credit 1790 first report
priority_1._note: Hamilton's first major report as Secretary of the Treasury — the founding document of American public finance. Also contained in Lodge Vol 4.

priority_1.title: Report on a National Bank
priority_1.author: Alexander Hamilton
priority_1.search_terms: hamilton report national bank 1790 secretary treasury
priority_1._note: The argument for the Bank of the United States; includes Hamilton's doctrine of implied powers. Also in Lodge collection.

priority_1.title: Report on Manufactures
priority_1.author: Alexander Hamilton
priority_1.search_terms: hamilton report on manufactures 1791 secretary treasury
priority_1._note: Hamilton's case for industrial policy and protective tariffs — his most visionary document and the one most vindicated by 19th century history. Also in Lodge collection.

priority_2.title: Works of Alexander Hamilton Vol 7 (Lodge Edition)
priority_2.author: Alexander Hamilton

priority_2.title: Works of Alexander Hamilton Vol 8 (Lodge Edition)
priority_2.author: Alexander Hamilton

priority_2.title: Works of Alexander Hamilton Vol 9 (Lodge Edition)
priority_2.author: Alexander Hamilton

priority_2.title: Works of Alexander Hamilton Vol 10 (Lodge Edition)
priority_2.author: Alexander Hamilton

priority_2.title: Works of Alexander Hamilton Vol 11 (Lodge Edition)
priority_2.author: Alexander Hamilton

priority_2.title: Works of Alexander Hamilton Vol 12 (Lodge Edition)
priority_2.author: Alexander Hamilton

priority_2.title: Observations on Certain Documents (Reynolds Pamphlet)
priority_2.author: Alexander Hamilton
priority_2.search_terms: hamilton reynolds pamphlet observations certain documents 1797
priority_2._note: Hamilton's remarkable self-published confession of adultery, written to defend his financial integrity against speculation charges. Extraordinary primary source for his psychology and his hierarchy of values — reputation for public probity above all else, including his marriage.

priority_2.title: The Letters of Alexander Hamilton — Military Correspondence
priority_2.author: Alexander Hamilton
priority_2.search_terms: hamilton letters correspondence military washington aide de camp revolutionary war
priority_2._note: Hamilton's wartime correspondence with Washington, Lafayette, and others. Shows the military mind behind the financial architect.

priority_2.title: Hamilton's Legal Opinions and Arguments
priority_2.author: Alexander Hamilton
priority_2.search_terms: hamilton legal opinions arguments attorney new york law
priority_2._note: Hamilton's legal writings from his New York law practice. The same structural rigor he brought to finance.

priority_2.title: The Works of Alexander Hamilton (1850 John C. Hamilton Edition)
priority_2.author: Alexander Hamilton
priority_2.search_terms: works of alexander hamilton john c hamilton 1850 7 volumes
priority_2._note: Earlier 7-volume edition compiled by Hamilton's son John C. Hamilton. Predates Lodge but includes valuable correspondence not in all later editions.

context_sources.title: Alexander Hamilton
context_sources.author: Ron Chernow
context_sources._comment: The definitive biography — 818 pages, basis for the Lin-Manuel Miranda musical. Chernow rehabilitated Hamilton from Jeffersonian caricature. Place in context/raw/ and run context_synthesizer.py --persona alexander-hamilton.

context_sources.title: The Quartet: Orchestrating the Second American Revolution, 1783-1789
context_sources.author: Joseph J. Ellis
context_sources._comment: Ellis on the four men (Hamilton, Madison, Jay, Washington) who engineered the Constitutional Convention and ratification. Places Hamilton's role in the constitutional moment precisely. Place in context/raw/.

discourse_sources.title: Memoir Correspondence and Miscellanies Vol 1 (Jefferson Letters)
discourse_sources.author: Thomas Jefferson
discourse_sources._note: Contains Jefferson's letters to Madison and others condemning Hamilton's financial system as monarchist corruption. Primary source for the anti-Hamilton argument. Embed in discourse collection.

discourse_sources.title: Memoir Correspondence and Miscellanies Vol 2 (Jefferson Letters)
discourse_sources.author: Thomas Jefferson
discourse_sources._note: Continued Jefferson correspondence attacking Hamiltonian policy and vision. Embed in discourse collection.

discourse_sources.title: Works of James Madison — Letters and Papers
discourse_sources.author: James Madison
discourse_sources.search_terms: works of james madison letters papers gaillard hunt congress
discourse_sources._note: Madison's political papers and correspondence — he was Hamilton's co-author on the Federalist and later his principal antagonist. His Federalist No. 10 and 51 provide the contrasting Madisonian vision of government. Embed in discourse collection.

discourse_sources.title: The Works of John Adams — Letters and Correspondence
discourse_sources.author: John Adams
discourse_sources.search_terms: works of john adams little brown charles francis adams correspondence
discourse_sources._note: Adams loathed Hamilton and wrote some of the most devastating personal critiques in American political history. The Letters to Timothy Pickering (1804) and his later correspondence are particularly sharp. Embed in discourse collection.
